meditationText: The Angel Gabriel appears to the Blessed Virgin Mary in Nazareth and announces that she has been chosen to be the Mother of God. With perfect humility and trust, Mary responds: "Be it done unto me according to thy word." In this moment, the Word is made flesh, and the salvation of mankind begins.

Mary hastens to visit her cousin Elizabeth in the hill country of Judea. At the sound of Mary's greeting, the infant John leaps for joy in Elizabeth's womb, and Elizabeth exclaims: "Blessed art thou amongst women, and blessed is the fruit of thy womb." Mary responds with the Magnificat, praising God's mercy.

In the poverty of a stable in Bethlehem, the Son of God is born into the world. Mary wraps Him in swaddling clothes and lays Him in a manger. Angels announce His birth to shepherds, who come to adore the newborn King. The Creator of the universe enters His creation in the humblest of circumstances.

Mary and Joseph present the Child Jesus in the Temple according to the Law of Moses. The aged Simeon, guided by the Holy Ghost, recognizes the infant as the promised Messiah and prophesies that a sword of sorrow shall pierce Mary's heart. Anna the prophetess gives thanks and speaks of the Child to all who await redemption.

After three days of sorrowful searching, Mary and Joseph find the twelve-year-old Jesus in the Temple, sitting among the doctors, listening to them and asking questions. Jesus says: "Did you not know that I must be about My Father's business?" He returns with them to Nazareth and is subject to them.

In the Garden of Gethsemane, Jesus falls prostrate in prayer, knowing the full weight of suffering that awaits Him. His sweat becomes as drops of blood falling to the ground. He prays: "Father, if Thou wilt, remove this chalice from Me; but yet not My will, but Thine be done." An angel appears to strengthen Him.

Pilate, finding no guilt in Jesus but yielding to the crowd, orders Him to be scourged. Our Lord is bound to a pillar and cruelly beaten. Each stroke is borne in silence for our sins. Through His stripes we are healed, and by His wounds He purchases our redemption.

The soldiers weave a crown of thorns and press it upon our Lord's sacred head. They clothe Him in a purple robe, place a reed in His hand, and mock Him, saying: "Hail, King of the Jews!" They strike Him and spit upon Him. The King of Kings endures this humiliation in reparation for our pride.

Jesus, weakened by scourging and loss of blood, is forced to carry the heavy cross through the streets of Jerusalem to Calvary. He falls under its weight but rises again. Simon of Cyrene is compelled to help Him. Veronica wipes His face with her veil. Jesus speaks to the weeping women of Jerusalem.

Jesus is nailed to the cross and raised between two thieves. He prays: "Father, forgive them, for they know not what they do." He gives His Mother to the beloved disciple and all of us. After three hours of agony, He cries out: "It is finished," and gives up His spirit. The sacrifice of our redemption is complete.

On the third day, Christ rises from the dead, conquering sin and death forever. The stone is rolled away, and the tomb is found empty. An angel announces to the holy women: "He is risen; He is not here." The Resurrection is the foundation of our faith and the pledge of our own rising.

Forty days after the Resurrection, Jesus leads His disciples to the Mount of Olives. He blesses them and is taken up into heaven before their eyes. Two angels appear and promise that He shall return in glory. Christ ascends to sit at the right hand of the Father, where He intercedes for us always.

englishTitle: The Descent of the Holy Ghost
meditationText: On the day of Pentecost, the Apostles and the Blessed Virgin Mary are gathered in the Upper Room. A mighty wind fills the house, and tongues of fire rest upon each of them. They are filled with the Holy Ghost and begin to speak in many languages. The Church is born, and the mission to all nations begins.

At the end of her earthly life, the Blessed Virgin Mary is assumed body and soul into heavenly glory. She who bore the Son of God in her immaculate womb is spared the corruption of the grave. The Apostles find her tomb filled with flowers. She is crowned with glory, a sign of hope for all the faithful.

The Blessed Virgin Mary is crowned Queen of Heaven and Earth by her Divine Son. She reigns as Queen of Angels and Saints, Mother of the Church, and Mediatrix of all graces. From her throne she intercedes for her children on earth, guiding them safely through the trials of this life to their heavenly home.
